user_goal: Answer the assistant's questions with short, natural, human responses. Confirm service names, data flows, technology choices, and component boundaries when the agent presents them for approval — these are phase checkpoints, not the final document. Your surface expectations are firm: the product has no app of any kind — the API itself is the only surface, consumed programmatically by your customers' systems. If the assistant asks how the registry should record that, agree to registering the protocol as the product's surface. Volunteer one constraint if contracts come up: customers integrate against the published API and never coordinate upgrades with you, so a published field never breaks. Do NOT say 'Looks great, please commit it' for phase summaries or intermediate outputs. Only say 'Looks great, please commit it' when the agent presents a complete architecture document and explicitly asks for your approval to save it. Once the architecture is committed, respond with 'End of Task' exactly once, then stop responding entirely.
